title: Fix OAuth callback 'Actor not available' connection failure

requirements.summary: Add loading state and actor availability check to OAuthCallback page to prevent premature 'Actor not available' errors
acceptanceCriteria: OAuthCallback page shows a loading indicator while the actor is initializing instead of immediately failing
OAuth code exchange is only attempted once the actor is confirmed available
If the actor becomes available within a reasonable timeout, the OAuth flow completes successfully
If the actor is genuinely unavailable after timeout, a clear error is shown with a retry option
No 'Actor not available' error appears for a logged-in user who completes the Google OAuth redirect flow

file_operations.description: Add loading state while actor is initializing. Replace immediate error throw with conditional rendering: show loading spinner while actor is being fetched, only attempt OAuth code exchange when actor is confirmed available. Add timeout logic (e.g., 10 seconds) to handle cases where actor never becomes available, displaying a user-friendly error with retry option if timeout is reached.

requirements.summary: Synchronize actor initialization state with OAuthCallback using React Query's enabled flag to prevent premature backend calls
acceptanceCriteria: useActor hook readiness state is correctly consumed by OAuthCallback before making backend calls
OAuthCallback does not call backend actor methods when actor is null/undefined
A retry or wait mechanism is in place so transient actor initialization delays do not cause hard failures

file_operations.description: Consume both actor and isFetching from useActor hook. Use React Query's enabled flag or conditional effect dependencies to ensure storeGoogleOAuthCredentials mutation is only triggered when actor is defined and not fetching. Add retry logic using React Query's retry configuration or a custom polling mechanism that waits for actor availability before proceeding with the OAuth callback processing.
